content.text: in the latest commit I moved the subcategories on the src/pages/OrganizationApplication/NameAndType/NameAndType.tsx page into the categories component. Lets write a quick playwright test to validate that this page is still working properly

payload.text:
**Analyzing account fetching and testing scenarios**

I'm untangling how `useGetAccount` depends on route params and context, realizing the new account page lacks an account ID so it doesn't fetch data, defaulting to initial values instead. I'm sketching a test plan that starts the new account page route, mocks network calls to avoid hitting real APIs, and verifies the category and subcategory selection behavior, while confirming translations and UI text. This way, I can ensure the test mimics the real page flow accurately.

summary.text:
**Analyzing account fetching and testing scenarios**

I'm untangling how `useGetAccount` depends on route params and context, realizing the new account page lacks an account ID so it doesn't fetch data, defaulting to initial values instead. I'm sketching a test plan that starts the new account page route, mocks network calls to avoid hitting real APIs, and verifies the category and subcategory selection behavior, while confirming translations and UI text. This way, I can ensure the test mimics the real page flow accurately.

payload.text:
**Analyzing network request hooks and test plan**

I'm exploring how the `AuthenticatedWrapper` expects a `csrfToken` in the `me` endpoint response and checking what other requests the app might make after user authentication. I want to intercept key API calls like `GET /user/v3/me`, `GET /user/v3/accounts`, and `POST /org/v3/accounts` to stub responses for testing the new account page. I'm also tracing how account creation triggers navigation via `useSteps` to understand the next step in the onboarding flow. This gives me a clear test plan focused on network hooking and response simulation.

summary.text:
**Analyzing network request hooks and test plan**

I'm exploring how the `AuthenticatedWrapper` expects a `csrfToken` in the `me` endpoint response and checking what other requests the app might make after user authentication. I want to intercept key API calls like `GET /user/v3/me`, `GET /user/v3/accounts`, and `POST /org/v3/accounts` to stub responses for testing the new account page. I'm also tracing how account creation triggers navigation via `useSteps` to understand the next step in the onboarding flow. This gives me a clear test plan focused on network hooking and response simulation.

payload.text:
**Analyzing account creation flow and test setup**

I'm examining how to handle partial account data responses to avoid runtime errors, focusing on key fields needed for navigation and step logic. I also need to intercept relevant network requests during testing to simulate backend behavior and verify UI elements like translated category labels on the application page.

summary.text:

payload.text:
**Planning API intercepts for tests**

I’m mapping out which API endpoints to intercept to ensure user and account data are properly mocked during tests—focusing on user info, account creation, and feature flags—so the queries reliant on these data run smoothly even when `accountId` is undefined on new account pages. I'll also check test guidelines before adding specs.

summary.text:
**Planning API intercepts for tests**

I’m mapping out which API endpoints to intercept to ensure user and account data are properly mocked during tests—focusing on user info, account creation, and feature flags—so the queries reliant on these data run smoothly even when `accountId` is undefined on new account pages. I'll also check test guidelines before adding specs.

# Anedot Playwright Testing

## Strategy

The idea behind the UI Automation tests is to replicate what the _user_ experiences when they are interacting with the application. Ideally, these tests are
end-to-end (E2E) in that they test the UI against a live backend (either locally or remotely). Currently we have decided to only test frontend user interaction as some limiting factors bar us from a full E2E testing suite (ie. test API routes, coordinated local/prod/ci DB, etc).

## Choice of Framework

We wanted to be able to test as many platforms as possible using a single framework (chrome, webkit, mobile, etc).
Playwright seemed to have the most active community and provided the best options for TypeScript at the time.

### [Playwright](https://playwright.dev/)

- Supports most major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Mobile viewports)
- Excellent out of the box support for TypeScript
- Concise syntax
- Reporting options/visual regression options

### Other Frameworks Considered

- [Cypress](https://www.cypress.io/)

  - Did not have solid support for Safari at the time (Jan 2024)

- [Webdriver.IO / Selenium](https://webdriver.io/docs/selenium-standalone-service/)
  - Supports most major browsers as well as device simulators
  - Verbose syntax
  - Requires a lot of boilerplate and setup/maintenance
  - Lacked out of the box support for TypeScript
  - Visual regression testing is in a state of limbo due to dependency collisions

## Installing Playwright

- Remove node_modules `rm -rf node_modules/`
- Reinstall packages `yarn install`
- Install playwright `yarn playwright install` (may need to add --with-deps flag if errors occur)
- (optional) Run `npx update-browserslist-db@latest` [Info Here](https://github.com/browserslist/update-db#readme)
- (optional) Install [Playwright Test for VSCode](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-playwright.playwright) to run in editor
- Run `yarn test:playwright` to check if test suite runs successfully

## Authoring Tests

### Fixtures

There are test _fixtures_ in `src/tests/fixtures` that extend and exports the `test` function and only exports the `expect` functions. These are reuseable steps or tests that can be used to provide micro-workflows for certain functions in the application (i.e., authentication). Additional fixtures can be added as seen fit and are advised to follow the syntax provided in `src/tests/fixtures` files.

The `test` function injects a fixture to be used within `test` functions (cannot be used in before/after hooks).

[Playwright Fixtures](https://playwright.dev/docs/test-fixtures)

### Actions

Please follow best practices noted in playwright documentation when locating and interaction with elements.

[Playwright Actions](https://playwright.dev/docs/input)

### Assertions

Use auto-waiting assertions as a priority.

[Playwright Assertions](https://playwright.dev/docs/test-assertions)

### Important Notes

- Public Action Page testing currently need `test.slow()` as the public page render has to load the entire bundle and will timeout on slower machines (ie. GH Actions).

### Example

Each test is wrapped in a `test` function and the `expect` function provides ways to validate components and elements on the screen. The `test` function takes an async callback and injects a [`Page`](https://playwright.dev/docs/api/class-page) object (that provides functions for navigating within the browser and locating elements on the screen) and any fixtures added in the fixtures folder.

```javascript
import { expect, test } from '../fixtures';

test('Action Pages', async ({ page, publicActionPage }) => {
  // Use injected fixture to mock action page API response
  await publicActionPage.mockActionPageGet({
    body: createDonationPageMock(),
    path: '**/public/v3/action_pages/playwright_test_page**',
  });

  // Go to the default page
  await page.goto('/washington/playwright_test_page');

  // Use this fixture to test that the action page API has been received
  await publicActionPage.testLoadedState();

  // make sure the page has loaded
  await expect(page.locator('.chakra-spinner')).toHaveCount(0);

  // make sure it is the correct page by checking text
  await expect(page.getByText('This is the Playwright Test Page')).toBeVisible();

  // Take a screenshot and compare it against a previously saved screenshot
  // This fails on the first run if the baseline has not been generated
  await page.screenshot({ path: 'testPage.png', fullPage: true });
  await expect(page).toHaveScreenshot('testPage.png');
});
```

## Running Tests

Tests run in headless mode by default.

```
yarn test:playwright

--headed                Run in headed browser
--ui                    Run in interactive UI mode
--debug                 Run debug mode (step by step)
--repeat-each=#         Repeat tests # of times
--project=chromium      Test against specific project (ex. `chromium`)
```

## Reporting

Playwright CI is implemented through GH actions workflow in the `.github/workflows/playwright.yml` file.

The generated report can be served/viewed.

```
yarn playwright show-report
```

## Other Notes

- Workers are set to 1 in the config as increasing workers is known to cause flakiness in testing, especially in CI environments.

payload.text:
**Preparing mock data and intercepts for tests**

I'm planning out mock responses and route interceptions for the spec tests, focusing on user data and API calls like accounts and feature toggles. I want to set up realistic user info including a CSRF token and handle necessary API routes to keep tests stable and aligned with expected interfaces. I'll check related files for cache refresh behavior next.

summary.text:
**Preparing mock data and intercepts for tests**

I'm planning out mock responses and route interceptions for the spec tests, focusing on user data and API calls like accounts and feature toggles. I want to set up realistic user info including a CSRF token and handle necessary API routes to keep tests stable and aligned with expected interfaces. I'll check related files for cache refresh behavior next.

payload.text:
**Designing API request interception**

I'm sorting out how to intercept various API calls during tests, like GET and POST to `/org/v3/accounts` and related feature flag endpoints, ensuring correct responses without conflicts. I’m planning careful method-based routing to handle fetches and mutations, prepping routes before navigation, and considering existing test fixtures for consistent setup, including handling React Query devtools to avoid UI overlap during interactions.

payload.text:
**Planning request interception and navigation checks**

I'm working out how to intercept and inspect the POST request to `/org/v3/accounts` using Playwright’s route and waitForRequest together, while also stubbing needed API responses like user auth and feature flags to ensure smooth page loading and state setup. I want to confirm correct post data, navigate properly after creation, and handle React Query Devtools visibility. I'll peek into related components like `PageTitle` to understand their fetch behavior when on `/accounts/application`.

payload.text:
**Analyzing test setup and intercept strategies**

I’m piecing together how to mock network requests for user, feature flags, and account data using Playwright routes, ensuring `csrfToken` integration and correct redirect behavior. I plan to verify form radio controls and their submission payloads carefully, checking how boolean/string values are handled, especially in subcategory and religious fields, while inspecting related schema code to confirm validation logic.

summary.text:
**Analyzing test setup and intercept strategies**

I’m piecing together how to mock network requests for user, feature flags, and account data using Playwright routes, ensuring `csrfToken` integration and correct redirect behavior. I plan to verify form radio controls and their submission payloads carefully, checking how boolean/string values are handled, especially in subcategory and religious fields, while inspecting related schema code to confirm validation logic.

payload.text:
**Designing account creation test setup**

I’m sketching how to test account creation with a `religious` field as a string sent in POST requests but possibly stored as a boolean. I plan to intercept POST requests in Playwright, capture and verify request bodies, and handle subsequent GET responses to reflect created account data consistently across routes. I need to prepare mocks, route handlers, and await requests properly to validate submission payloads and state transitions. Let’s organize test files and helper functions to keep things tidy.

summary.text:
**Designing account creation test setup**

I’m sketching how to test account creation with a `religious` field as a string sent in POST requests but possibly stored as a boolean. I plan to intercept POST requests in Playwright, capture and verify request bodies, and handle subsequent GET responses to reflect created account data consistently across routes. I need to prepare mocks, route handlers, and await requests properly to validate submission payloads and state transitions. Let’s organize test files and helper functions to keep things tidy.

payload.text:
**Planning network interception for onboarding and accounts**

I’m figuring out how to intercept specific API calls like onboarding surveys and bank accounts, including requests with certain query parameters for account IDs. I’ll provide sample empty responses for these endpoints and think about how feature flags and rollbar logging might interact with fetch requests in this context. I also want to check how RollbarWrapper loads scripts to understand potential side effects.

summary.text:
**Planning network interception for onboarding and accounts**

I’m figuring out how to intercept specific API calls like onboarding surveys and bank accounts, including requests with certain query parameters for account IDs. I’ll provide sample empty responses for these endpoints and think about how feature flags and rollbar logging might interact with fetch requests in this context. I also want to check how RollbarWrapper loads scripts to understand potential side effects.

payload.text:
**Analyzing API intercepts and test setup**

I'm examining all the network requests that need interception for the tests, including feature flags, account info, and bank accounts. I want to ensure responses match expected API shapes, especially for paginated data, before crafting the Playwright spec with properly registered routes and handling test-specific submitted data.

summary.text:
**Analyzing API intercepts and test setup**

I'm examining all the network requests that need interception for the tests, including feature flags, account info, and bank accounts. I want to ensure responses match expected API shapes, especially for paginated data, before crafting the Playwright spec with properly registered routes and handling test-specific submitted data.
